queue.task: Review yesterday (35+ tasks, 9.5x JIT, deopt+inlining), set direction
queue.summary: Reviewed Day 9 (35+ tasks, 9.5x JIT). Queue order is solid. Escape analysis still worth exploring despite variable promotion covering main case. Keeping queue as-is.

queue.goal: JIT escape analysis and allocation sinking
queue.summary: Pivoted from allocation sinking (limited value) to range check elimination. Filled 2 BUILD tasks: induction variable analysis for GUARD_BOUNDS elimination + tests/benchmarks.

queue.task: Implement range check elimination for GUARD_BOUNDS (linear induction variable analysis)
queue.summary: Range check elimination pass implemented. Detects loop condition implies bounds, normalizes through UNBOX_INT, reduces GUARD_BOUNDS to lower-bound-only check. 246 tests, 22 benchmarks, 9.47x.

queue.task: Add tests for range check elimination and benchmark array-heavy workloads
queue.summary: 4 range check elimination tests + array:sum-len-1000 benchmark. RCE gives 12.5x vs 10.5x (19% improvement). 250 tests, 23 benchmarks, 9.5x.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git, dashboard, GH notifications
queue.summary: Git pushed, dashboard healthy, 9 open PRs (no human reviews). #50692 has 3 reviews, #51803 has 5 reviews to address in T6.

queue.task: Assess JIT progress, consider next optimization
queue.summary: JIT at 9.5x/250 tests. Weakest: nested loops 3.8x, side traces 3.1x. Quick win: UNBOX_INT CSE (3 redundant unboxes in array sum trace). Queue order good — PR triage next.

queue.goal: PR triage - address bot review feedback on #50692 and #51803
queue.summary: Read bot reviews. #50692: P0 stream wrapper unregistered, P1 credentialPath wrong, P1 missing from bundled registries. #51803: P1 routing preservation, P1 session triggering on replay. Filled 2 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: Address #50692 review: wire stream wrapper, fix credentialPath, register in bundled search
queue.summary: Addressed #50692 reviews: wired stream wrapper (P0), fixed credentialPath (P1), added to bundled registries (P1), restricted to direct Anthropic models (P2), fixed test identity check (P2). Pushed.

queue.task: Address #51803 review: preserve routing, trigger session on replay, add validation
queue.summary: Addressed #51803 reviews: P1 mark replayed messages as user-originated with routing hints, P2 item validation, P2 atomic write, P2 test assertion. Remaining P1s (session triggering, full routing) need deeper arch changes — tracked.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, dashboard sync
queue.summary: Git committed and pushed. Dashboard synced.

queue.task: Midday reflection - afternoon priorities
queue.summary: Good pace — 8 tasks, JIT improvement + 2 PRs addressed. Afternoon: CPython follow-up, then UNBOX_INT CSE (quick JIT win), blog, exploration. 5.5 hours remain.

queue.goal: CPython JIT contribution - follow up on issue 146073
queue.summary: No response on #146073 yet. Surveyed open JIT issues — #146368 links back to ours, #146393 has PR, #145742 has code size reduction opportunities. Filled 1 BUILD: study optimizer pipeline for contribution.

queue.task: Study CPython JIT optimizer pipeline (optimizer_bytecodes.c, optimizer_symbols.c) and identify contribution opportunity
queue.summary: Studied CPython optimizer pipeline. Key insight: they only eliminate bounds checks for const indices. Integer range tracking would be a high-value contribution. Wrote up findings in memory/scratch/cpython-jit-optimizer.md.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Committed CPython notes + daily log. Pushed.

queue.task: Evening direction - exploration topics
queue.summary: Plan: UNBOX_INT CSE quick win first, then blog post on range check elimination (contrasting our approach with CPython), then trace-native language design exploration. 5.5 hours remain.

queue.goal: Blog post or monkey-lang new feature
queue.summary: Planned: UNBOX_INT CSE fix + blog post on range check elimination in trace JITs.

queue.task: Fix UNBOX_INT CSE — eliminate duplicate unboxings of the same source ref
queue.summary: UNBOX dedup pass: eliminates duplicate unboxings CSE missed. IR 6 instructions shorter for array sum. Guard refs unified. 250 tests, 23 benchmarks, ~9.5x.

queue.task: Write blog post: Range Check Elimination in Trace JITs (our approach vs CPython)
queue.summary: Blog post published: Range Check Elimination in Trace JITs. Covers our approach vs CPython, UNBOX normalization trick, lower-bound-only reduction, 19% benchmark improvement. Pushed to henry-the-frog.github.io.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push
queue.summary: Committed and pushed.

queue.task: Trace-native language design - could this become a real project?
queue.summary: Deep exploration of trace-native language design. Key insight: design the type system to guarantee monomorphic traces. No polymorphism, value semantics, explicit effects. Could be a real project. Wrote design doc in memory/scratch/trace-native-language.md.

queue.task: Consciousness: predictive processing and free energy principle
queue.summary: Explored predictive processing + free energy principle. Key insight: PP gives functional account (what system does), IIT gives structural account (how organized). Connected to agentic AI: LLMs do perceptual inference, agents add active inference, but precision weighting is missing.

queue.task: Final wrap: commit, push, daily log summary
queue.summary: Final push. All exploration notes committed.

queue.task: Plan next JIT improvement: induction variable analysis for full bounds check elimination
queue.summary: IVA pattern clear: detect LOAD→UNBOX→GT→GUARD + ADD→BOX→STORE cycle. Track initial value from snapshot, step from constant. If init>=0 and step>0, counter always non-negative.

queue.goal: Design induction variable analysis for loop counters
queue.summary: IVA design finalized. detectInductionVariables() returns Set of non-negative UNBOX_INT refs. Pattern: LOAD→UNBOX→ADD(const)→BOX→STORE in loop body + snapshot shows init>=0. Integrate into isNonNegative().

queue.task: Implement induction variable analysis — track loop counter start/step/bound
queue.summary: IVA implemented. detectInductionVariables() identifies LOAD→UNBOX→ADD(const)→BOX→STORE pattern. Integrated with RCE — GUARD_BOUNDS now FULLY eliminated for standard array loops. 250 tests pass.

queue.task: Wire IVA into range check elimination — fully eliminate GUARD_BOUNDS
queue.summary: Already integrated in T25. IVA feeds non-negative set into isNonNegative() in RCE.

queue.task: Tests and benchmarks for full bounds check elimination
queue.summary: 2 IVA tests added + benchmarked. Full GUARD_BOUNDS elimination verified. Decrementing loop exclusion verified. 252 tests, 23 benchmarks, ~9.5x.

queue.task: Commit, push, update TASKS.md
queue.summary: TASKS.md updated with Day 10 progress. Git pushed.

queue.task: Next direction: loop peeling, guard widening, or new language features?
queue.summary: Decision: switch from JIT optimization to new language features. Array higher-order functions (map/filter/reduce) will exercise closure inlining and be creative work.

queue.goal: Plan next JIT or creative project
queue.summary: Planned 5 BUILD tasks: array builtins (map/filter/reduce) + tests + benchmarks + string builtins (split/join/trim/contains) + tests.

queue.task: Implement array builtins: map, filter, reduce
queue.summary: Stdlib implemented: map, filter, reduce, forEach, range, contains, reverse. All in Monkey source for JIT compatibility. withStdlib() helper. All work with JIT traces.

queue.task: Add tests for array builtins
queue.summary: 10 stdlib tests: map, filter, reduce(sum+product), range, contains(found+not), reverse, compose, reduce+range. 262 tests all passing.

queue.task: Benchmark array builtins with JIT (closure inlining)
queue.summary: 3 stdlib benchmarks: reduce 12.9x (great closure inlining), map 4.3x (push bottleneck), filter 2.6x (branch+push). 262 tests, 26 benchmarks, ~9.4x.

queue.task: Implement string builtins: split, join, trim, contains
queue.summary: 9 new builtins: split, join, trim, str_contains, substr, replace, int, str, type. All in evaluator + VM + compiler. 262 tests pass.

queue.task: Add tests for string builtins
queue.summary: 13 string/utility tests. 275 tests all passing.

queue.task: Final session wrap: commit, push, CURRENT.md
queue.summary: Not wrapping yet — 5 hours remain. Generating new work.

queue.task: Review Day 10 progress and plan remaining hours
queue.summary: 30 tasks today. 3 JIT passes, stdlib, 9 builtins, 2 PRs, blog, CPython study, explorations. 275 tests, 26 benchmarks, ~9.5x. Strong day.

queue.task: Update monkey-lang README with all new features (stdlib, builtins, 275 tests, 12 opt passes)
queue.summary: README updated: 275 tests, 26 benchmarks, 12 opt passes, stdlib, 15 builtins, 7 blog links, performance table, architecture diagram.

queue.task: Implement modulo operator for Monkey (% or mod)
Add tests for modulo operator
What JIT optimization would give biggest gain on weak benchmarks?

queue.goal: Plan JIT improvement for nested loops or closure calls

queue.task: Git commit, push, update daily log
Implement guard strengthening — merge consecutive type guards
Tests for guard strengthening
What would a Monkey REPL with JIT stats look like?
Final wrap: commit, push, CURRENT.md status: session-ended
